title: Entity 10
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Entity 10: "Skin-Stealers /// Capgras Delusion"}} __NOTOC__ {{Background|file=Nottobetrusted.png}} {{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0,0.999999999); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(0, 0, 0,0.9999999999); --theme-page-text-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-border-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1: #ffffff; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1--rgb: 255,255,255; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-community-header-color: #FFFFFF; } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(10, 10, 10,0.999999999); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(10, 10, 10,0.9999999999); --theme-page-text-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-border-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1: #ffffff; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1--rgb: 255,255,255; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-community-header-color: #FFFFFF; } li{ --theme-page-text-color: #FFFFFF; } }} {{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-link-color: #808080 } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-link-color: #808080; } }}

⇊ click to continue. ⇊
 {{Blurimage|file=Bloodrunsred.png|align=center|width=600px|caption=An informational tape by the Anti-Entity Group, produced in 1994.}}

{{Blurimage|file=shadowgun.png|align=center|width=600px|caption=...}} "Alright, time to see if any of you are Skin-Stealers." A man, around 6 feet tall and visibly malnourished, aimed a gun at the other twenty people in the cramped [[Level 1|warehouse]] room. Pacing throughout the chamber, his hand shook with anxiety even with the powerful firearm he wielded. For many here, the tension was suffocating. For Micheal, the man holding the gun, it was the weekly check. Walking slowly towards a young boy, he brandished a makeshift knife made of rusty metal. Throwing him on the floor, Micheal put his boot on the kid's arm to restrain him, inching closer towards his hand with the knife. Raising his hand to make a swing, he slashed at the boy's hand, causing it to bleed out. Red blood. The child would live today. Micheal repeated the process with the others. Ten people in, and all were still human so far. The next one up was a woman, around 50 years old. She had arrived at the colony a couple weeks back. Apparently, she'd been part of some [[Backrooms Colonists|big group]] out there in the Backrooms, until one day she returned to her colony after a resource gathering mission and everyone had disappeared. Repeating the process once more with the others, Micheal noticed a beige shape moving towards him in a flash, along with a sharp pain in his back. In an instant, he turned around and shot at the attacker-
'''''BANG! BANG! BANG!'''''
 Three bodies lay on the floor, along with the corpse of his attacker: another frail man not much unlike him, armed with yet another makeshift knife. The others must've caught a stray bullet. Micheal checked the bullet wound on the person who had assaulted him. Red blood; he was still a human. "Looks like Skin-Stealers aren't the only traitors around here", Micheal said with a smirk on his face, turning around to the young boy from before. "You were his kid, right? Now all of you remember, these rules are all to keep us safe and happy. You like being safe and happy, right?" Raising his pistol towards the child, Micheal continued. "Now let this be an example of what happens when someone gets the idea that they're above my rules."
'''''BANG'''''
 One last shot rang through the room. Red blood. ==≪ Survival Guide ≫== * Examine the blood of your companions at least once per day. If it appears translucent, take the appropriate measures. * Check regularly for any differences in the appearances or behavior of your companions. * If you come across a colony which seems to be overly generous, do not trust them. Whether they are still human or not, good intentions are unlikely. * Show no mercy. They are not your friends or your family anymore. Do not hesitate to kill them. * If you are cornered, do not be taken alive. ==≪ Overview ≫== {{Blurimage|caption=The revealed appearance of a Skin-Stealer. Photographer is presumed either dead or forsaken.|file=Nottobetrusted.png}} '''Skin Stealers''', bearing the scientific name ''Homo capgras'' and classified as '''Entity 10''', are a species of predatory beings with polymorphic capabilities. Common in levels with significant human and/or [[Entity 9|other sapient]] presence, they have posed a notable threat to human civilization within the Backrooms. Skin Stealers are solo hunters, often employing regular use of suggestion and shapeshifting to lure out prey or groups of prey for consumption/assimilation. The population of Skin-Stealers remains unknown, though efforts from the AEA have consistently thinned down the population. ==≪ Biology ≫== Each Skin-Stealer possesses two forms in question: their disguise and their true form. Due to the preservation of much of the host's skin during the reproduction process, their disguise takes the appearance of their deceased host, albeit with some minor differences. Proportions are often changed slightly due to the consumption of all former internal organs, and although they inherit the memories of their victim, behavioral patterns can often seem visibly "off". In contrast, their "true" formDue to their polymorphic nature, Skin-Stealers do not possess any one "true" appearance. This is instead used to refer to any form the Skin-Stealer takes aside from its disguise. appears as a dark beige-colored, vaguely humanoid being with varying appearance. Often, instances of the entity form sharp appendages or claws to inflict maximum damage on any victim: however this comes with a disadvantage: any newly shifted body mass is unstable and fragile for a period of time, allowing any attacker to cause extreme injury while aiming at these body parts. While externally Skin-Stealers look alike to wanderers, autopsy reports have shown that the internals of the entities are vastly different. Skin-Stealers do not posses a standard digestive or respiratory system; the slight movements associated with breathing, swallowing, and chewing, are all controlled by a secondary musculature system that acts more as a collective organ to simulate the behaviorBecause of the possession-based hunting practices of Skin-Stealers, it is unknown how the fake organs are created. The heart of the entity remains in the same place as most humans; the left side of the sternum. These hearts also behave no differently to a human heart, and have shown to be effective against incapacitating a Skin-Stealer if the need arises. The blood vessels among the rest of the vascular system does not change either. To protect their organs and muscles, Skin-Stealers have developed a denser and tougher type of skin through large quantities of retinol. The entire body of a Skin-Stealer can change the amount of retinol depending on environmental factors and fitting into its host, such as adding more skin to replace arm muscles or adding extra around the legs to protect against hostile aquatic life. ==≪ Reproduction & Virology ≫== In order to successfully hunt and possess others, Skin-Stealers produce cells to pass on to others. These cells atomically provide information on how to convert wanderers into Skin-Stealers, and if enough are pushed into the wanderer, the turning process happens within hours. Otherwise, wanderers enter a mixed-skin state, based on the amount of attack cells present result in certain outcomes. {| class="article-table" style="border:2px solid white" ! style="padding:10px 10px; border-right:1px solid white"| 0 - 50k cells ! style="padding:10px 10px"| Attack cells are killed off with minimal harm to the wanderer's body. |- ! style="padding:10px 10px; border-right:1px solid white"| 50k - 150k cells ! style="padding:10px 10px"| Wanderer left is semi-serious condition, though emergency medical care is not required. |- ! style="padding:10px 10px; border-right:1px solid white"| 150k - 250k cells ! style="padding:10px 10px"| Emergency care necessary, and disfigurement is possible. |- ! style="padding:10px 10px; border-right:1px solid white"| 250k - 400k cells ! style="padding:10px 10px"| Permanent disfigurement highly likely, mercy kill may be the best option. |- ! style="padding:10px 10px; border-right:1px solid white"| 400k - 500k cells ! style="padding:10px 10px"| Wanderer left mostly turned, and will often be consumed by Skin-Stealers instead of being left to die. |- ! style="padding:10px 10px; border-right:1px solid white"| 500k + cells ! style="padding:10px 10px"| Full turn is extremely likely. |} {{Blurimage|file=TorsoExplosion.png|width=250px|caption=A warning sign placed in a skin-stealer dense section of [[Level 9]].|align=left}} Spread of Skin-Stealers cells typically only happens through contact with internal systems; obtaining these cells is commonly done through the sharing of saliva from either kissing, spit, vomit, or ███████████████████. A Skin-Stealer, when engaging in one of these ways, will produce saliva and attack cells through glands in their oral cavity and pharynx and push them into the others mouth, blood, or other exposed orifice. During the turning process, the newly produced cells will essentially eat the wanderer alive from the inside in a quite painful manner, preserving only two organs: the skin (to fuse with the newly created body as a disguise) and the brain (to siphon memories and knowledge from). ==≪ Behavior ≫== Skin-Stealers are stealthy and cunning predators. Although humans (and [[Entity 9|Facelings]]) are notable prey to them, they most commonly turn to other entities as a form of sustenance, due to their much greater frequency. When hunting an entity, Skin-Stealers will wander around a level disguised as a human, seeking to lure in entities attempting to predate on a human. When an entity gives chase, the Skin-Stealer will morph into its true form, seeking to take the entity by surprise and kill it swiftly. After their prey has expired, the Skin-Stealer will consume it, afterwards shifting back into its human disguise. Conversely, a Skin-Stealer's interactions with sapients tend to be much more varied; some prefer to kill a wanderer quickly while their guard is lowered, while some prefer to follow them to a settlement and look for more suitable prey there. Skin-Stealers have been reported to linger inside colonies for years on end, often picking off each member one by one and using their victims to assuage suspicion by other members of the colony. However, there is one saving grace when it comes to their behavior: since Skin-Stealers are largely solitary entities, the chance of them collaborating with each other to form larger groups is thankfully rare. ==≪ Discovery & History ≫== While first recorded in the 1600s, many have claimed to see individuals possessed by demons across the backrooms, with oral reports first surfacing hundreds of years prior. The earliest document was discovered in [[Level 906]] and was dated to be written in 1609, and has been translated to fit within modern language: {{Quote|Charles came back to me after several weeks of silence. I reasonably assumed him to be dead or otherwise disgraced by our populous. His return was met initially with concern, as he had left with two friends and returned alone. Many suspected him to have cannibalized them, though as I was able to find him traveling through the complex, I initiated conversation.

What started as surprise seemed to brush away quickly as he realized it was me talking to him. Though the moments still were odd, as he stared at me very intently. Then his attention went to my features, trying to find anything strange. Yet he relaxed, satisfied without confirmation, and finally spoke to me. We talked for mixed moments about by business, and then his plans to move to one of the other camps. We did get to the topic of his friends, more or less by my own force. I don't recall what I asked him, but he did give a response.

Charles told me that one of his friends was cursed by some sort of spirit. Passed to Charles' friend by another host, none of them knew who passed it to... Ivan. I think that's Charles' friend. Anyway he didn't know how Ivan got possessed, but the next time he and PatrickCharles' 3rd friend, considered dead or infected found Ivan, he looked like he got into a fight. Ivan brushed it off, so they kept going. Charles went into a lot about how he was able to tell it wasn't Ivan, but I can't remember any of it.

But his friend, Patrick, didn't see the issues, and brushed Charles off. So when Ivan and Patrick suddenly separated with Charles, he figured out Patrick was attacked by this demon too, and ran. Charles told me that both chased after him, though when I asked him later, it was only Ivan with blood on his lips. He started breaking down after that, reciting some old Latin prayers. I stopped asking him after that. |Suez, David. "The Story of Charles." Alters of the Sapiens, 1609, p. 114.}}
[[File:thefool.png|center|300px]]
Pisanthrophobia:
≼ "The Fool, the Farce, and the Traitor" ≽
--------- {{Blurimage|caption=''Emily's Face'', a well-known depiction of a Skin-Stealer which has been preserved for decades. Ironically, the original artist's identity has been lost to time.|file=emily.png}} Throughout my travels in the Backrooms, there has been one subject which continues to fascinate me, despite the wonders and horrors I have bore witness to in this purgatory: namely, Skin-Stealers. Beasts that take the shape of man, stripping all that we are bare, and replacing it with a hollow facsimile. In the Frontrooms, I remember hearing about a sort of mental disease where a person would begin to believed their loved one had been replaced by an outside force. ''Capgras Syndrome'', I believe it was called. Those who had it would often attempt to threaten the people they thought were impostors, obsessing over exposing them to the point of driving themselves mad. I believe that even though Skin-Stealers and their kind prey off of trust to find their victims, the distrust they cause is their true danger. Take, for example, a famous case told throughout the halls of the Backrooms: the micronation of New Doria, located in [[Level 1]] and led by a single man named Micheal along with his clique of armed guards. The colony had a rule in place to check the blood of each wanderer on a weekly basis, in order to ensure that they were not a Skin-Stealer. At first things went well, but eventually the man in charge got power hungry. Started to lose his grip on himself. As the stories say, Micheal soon started killing people who weren't Skin-Stealers during the weekly checks, just to assert his power. After a couple years, once someone finally figured out how to pick the lock of his private bedroom, they cut his throat open and left him to die, silently fighting the waning air. While many knew of his injuries, it seemed none were willing to help. For many, he had become an imposter of himself, rendered a husk of the intelligent leader he once was. And when dawn rises, they checked Micheal's corpse. The residents saw blood that ran clear. He had become his greatest enemy, and the surviving men and women, who knew their blood to be of crimson stain, chose to act. Micheal's guards were jumps, their throats slit and left blood to pour. Stricken by their fear, the residents watched as each of them did not soak the blade in red, but in the translucent fluid of the Skin Stealers. When I arrived at New Doria, it was a ghost town. The wanderers once filling the micronation with life lay the floor with their corpses. Some surrounded in a pool of red, but many, no color at all. Every step I took, I felt a shiver down my spine. My body warning me that someone was still out there. Watching and waiting to see if my blood was crimson too.
